storyInfo: Rankin attempts to take responsibility for the fire, but the surviving villagers refuse to allow it. The true culprit is judged and executed, resulting in a split among the miners. Vetochki, who chose to stay, begins to question herself in her sorrow.

attributes.text: Snow is falling on his head.
It turns into boiling water, into endless cries, into the weight of the dead.
Hands are not enough to sweep it away. It is wet, heavy, sinking...
Until it crushes those who are inside, those who are still living.

attributes.content: (Humming)
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: Babushka, h-how are you feeling?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: (Humming)
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: ...Tick, tock.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Tick, tock.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: (Humming)
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: The clock, ticks, tocks.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: The clock, doesn't wait, for anyone.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: But we, shouldn't sing?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Let's... l-let's go.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: No more survivors here.
attributes.name: Tired Miner

attributes.content: I don't think I have the strength to stand. Are you there, Lidiya? I could use a hand—
attributes.name: Tired Miner

attributes.content: Lidiya?
attributes.name: Tired Miner

attributes.content: Over there.
attributes.name: Calm Miner

attributes.content: Someone's coming.
attributes.name: Calm Miner

attributes.content: Rankin...
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: The fire's gone out.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Yes, it went out a while ago. Some survivors found us. We must do something.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Are you going to make Hawley kneel here forever? Even if he went mad and did all this, is it really necessary to punish him this way?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: The fire's gone out, and it's getting cold. He'll die.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Yes, he'll die. But...
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Not yet.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Rankin looks toward the entrance to the village.
The fence that used to be there was designed to stop foraging tuskbeasts, not anyone determined enough to enter, whether their intentions were benevolent, or malicious.
Scattered figures begin to emerge from behind the remains of the fence.
They look this way hesitantly, then walk away timidly.
Rankin sounds almost like he is muttering to himself—
I was wrong, Nikto.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: What do you mean?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: I was wrong.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: In what way?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Hawley wasn't mad. He wasn't shouting for Vetochki's babushka, or to his dead son. He was shouting at me.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: '...I have committed murder, not for food, nor for water, nor for abode.'
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: 'But for those who walk with me, for the salvation of their souls.'
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I didn't hear anything. Even if he did say it, it's just the kind of prayer that Vetochki and her babushka pray all the time.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: It's not like they started worshipping the Yurodstvo and the Tsar yesterday.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Yurodstvo? Tsar? No, not distant or ethereal things like that. He was trying to save my soul, even after I made him kill his own son.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: But what is a soul, even? Who can prove that it exists? Is it more precious than mortal life? It's ridiculous... silly!
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I shouldn't have forced him. But now, the worst has happened. If he did what he did before in order to fulfill my wishes, then it was for the same reason that he set this fire.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: No, it's nonsense, listen to me—
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: It might as well have been me who set this fire.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I must pay for my crimes.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Don't be a fool, Rankin!
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: I'll keep this brief.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: What... what else do you have to say?
attributes.name: Surviving Villager

attributes.content: We gave you food and water.
attributes.name: Surviving Villager

attributes.content: Is THIS how you repay us?
attributes.name: Surviving Villager

attributes.content: No.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Are you here to chase us away, or to finish what you started?
attributes.name: Surviving Villager

attributes.content: I want you to witness the perpetrator face justice.
attributes.name: Rankin
Surviving Villager
attributes.content: Ha... haha...

attributes.name: Surviving Villager
attributes.content: Hahahahaha...
Perpetrator? Justice? Will you kill all the arsonists, killers, and robbers in front of me?
attributes.name: Surviving Villager

attributes.content: No, you won't. Otherwise, you wouldn't be coming to talk to me while armed.
attributes.name: Surviving Villager

attributes.content: I'll pretend I didn't hear anything. Leave, unless you're trying to wipe us out.
attributes.name: Surviving Villager

attributes.content: Never come back again.
attributes.name: Surviving Villager

attributes.content: Rankin puts down his pickaxe and all other weapons, then raises both hands in the air.
Rankin!
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Stay back, Nikto.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: You still have your Arts...
attributes.name: Surviving Villager

attributes.content: Right here. The culprit is right here. You can do what you wish with him.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: What?
attributes.name: Surviving Villager

attributes.content: I've put down my weapon. I won't use any Arts.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: You can do what you wish.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I see. You would shield the others with your own life.
attributes.name: Surviving Villager

attributes.content: I've never been good with numbers... but I can tell that's not a fair deal.
attributes.name: Surviving Villager

attributes.content: I'm right here. I won't fight back.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I don't want you! We don't want you! You weren't here when the fire started!
attributes.name: Surviving Villager

attributes.content: I want the one who set the fire, the one who robbed my home and burned it down—
attributes.name: Surviving Villager

attributes.content: I want the one who killed my wife!
attributes.name: Surviving Villager

attributes.content: Even if I was the reason for his violence?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: It was my arrogance that put the others in a desperate situation. It was I who forced him down the path of violence.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: You should kill me.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: No.
attributes.name: Surviving Villager

attributes.content: Listen to me—
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: We want him. We don't want anyone else.
attributes.name: Surviving Villager

attributes.content: A small space has been cleared in the rubble to serve as an impromptu execution ground.
A crowd has gathered to watch. It is as though they cannot help themselves, like huddling around a fire in the winter night, yet fearful that its warmth might burn.
I still don't understand. Why did Hawley set the fire?
attributes.name: Tired Miner

attributes.content: Why would he do this to a village that helped us?
attributes.name: Tired Miner

attributes.content: He's mad.
attributes.name: Calm Miner

attributes.content: He was never the same again after his son died.
attributes.name: Calm Miner

attributes.content: But—maybe he didn't set the fire? Rankin wanted to satisfy the villagers, and Hawley happened to be sick.
attributes.name: Tired Miner

attributes.content: The Hawley I knew would never do something like that...
attributes.name: Tired Miner

attributes.content: I don't know, but I trust Rankin. Didn't you know? He even tried to trade his life for Hawley's.
attributes.name: Calm Miner

attributes.content: Thankfully, the villagers wanted the real culprit, not a good person trying to take his place.
attributes.name: Calm Miner

attributes.content: ...It hasn't started yet, but I can't watch...
attributes.name: Tired Miner

attributes.content: I-I'll be over there. I need some rest.
attributes.name: Tired Miner

attributes.content: Stop, Rankin's talking.
attributes.name: Calm Miner

attributes.content: Pretty much everyone's here.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: (Mutters) ...share...
attributes.name: Hawley

attributes.content: (Mutters) Pain...
attributes.name: Hawley

attributes.content: I have a few words to say before I execute this man. I'm not trying to defend him, or seek forgiveness from the victims.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Yes, he deserves to die.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: He betrayed kindness and trust. He placed all of us in danger. He harmed the innocent, in direct violation of our original intent, which was simply to survive.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Death may be inevitable on the path we chose, but it should never have happened this way.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Watch. Remember the lesson, and remember that the one about to take the life of this man is the one least qualified to do so.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I am executing him, on the wishes of the victims, and on my own decision.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: This will be the last decision I make as leader. No one should bear the burden of killing his own comrade. Therefore...
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Repent...
attributes.name: Hawley

attributes.content: Sin.
attributes.name: Hawley

attributes.content: The snow has piled up on Hawley's shoulder. He kneels on the ground, as though he did not hear any of what Rankin said, and puts his hands together in front of his chest in a gesture of piety.
He looks up, and exposes his neck to the executioner.
Yes... I must repent my sins. I shall do the deed.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: (Whisper) Do vstrechi, Hawley.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Gah!
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Damn... I thought I could at least put this mirror together. Now it's gotten even more broken.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Talk about unlucky.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Can't we at least take Hawley's body, Rankin?
attributes.name: Tired Miner

attributes.content: No.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: What use do they have for a dead body? Do they want to leave it out in the wilds...
attributes.name: Tired Miner

attributes.content: We're not in any position to negotiate.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Besides, all of you may come to the same end if you continue to follow me.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: What do you mean?
attributes.name: Tired Miner

attributes.content: Who do we follow, if not you? Now's not the time for jokes.
attributes.name: Tired Miner

attributes.content: It's not a joke. It's a decision.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: A decision that I already talked about.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Rankin turns around. The tired, unkempt crowd immediately gather around him, simply because he is here.
They look towards their leader, blankly waiting for the next instruction.
Do we keep going south, Rankin?
attributes.name: Dazed Miner

attributes.content: I don't have any strength left. I just wanted a roof over my head that could keep the snow away, but we don't even have that anymore, do we?
attributes.name: Dazed Miner

attributes.content: We'll die if we keep going...
attributes.name: Dazed Miner

attributes.content: We'll die if we stay.
attributes.name: Dazed Miner

attributes.content: I couldn't find them, Rankin.
attributes.name: Sad Miner

attributes.content: Pavel and Bogina... I never found them, not even their bodies. I'm sorry.
attributes.name: Sad Miner

attributes.content: The villagers didn't want me anywhere near them, not even when I put down my pickaxe. They're afraid. You could see it on their faces from a mile away.
attributes.name: Sad Miner

attributes.content: I didn't want to threaten them. Let's go, Rankin.
attributes.name: Sad Miner

attributes.content: Take us away.
attributes.name: Sad Miner

attributes.content: Rankin scans each face in the crowd, whether they spoke or not.
I'm sorry, friends.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Rankin?
attributes.name: Sad Miner

attributes.content: I can't decide your path going forward.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: As I said, it was not Hawley who should have been punished; it was me.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I was the one who drove him to madness and violence. It was my decisions that drove him to desperation.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Before this happened, I didn't even realize that not everyone was ready to look death in the eye.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: There's no longer any doubt. I don't have what it takes to be a leader. My last and best act as leader is to relinquish this position...
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: And give you back the power of choice.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Silence.
Long silence.
No one answers. Hardly anyone even breathes, not so much a twitch on their faces.
All who hear the decision are silent, until a trembling voice emerges from the end of the procession.
You're... abandoning... us?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: No, Vetochki.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Then, what? What you said... how do we, understand it?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: The crowd parts for the one who asked the question.
Vetochki does not move forward. She stands where she is, her fists clenched so hard that her knuckles have turned white.
You say, you won't lead, anymore! After we've come, so far?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Y-You're abandoning, all of us.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: I'm not! I must do this, before things get worse for everyone.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: No more lives must be lost because of my foolish decisions. We didn't escape from the mines, that hellhole, for this.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: H-Hellhole? But it's, my home.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: What do you mean?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: The mines, mining district, is my... home.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: It was, many people's, homes.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Home? I thought you all wanted out, like me.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: It's because, of trust. We trust, you—Rankin.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: You... you didn't, know?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: I thought you chose to leave because you've wanted to for a long time.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: You killed, the black bugs. You stood up, and said we, should leave, so... we left.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: You caught Hawley, killed him, and said, it was wrong, to leave. You said, it is, a choice.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: I-I don't understand! What are we, to you?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Friends, family... it's complicated and hard to define. But it's exactly because you're important to me that—
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: This is, the home, of the villagers.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Maybe they can, rebuild the village. What about us? Where do... we go?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: You took us, so far. If you don't, lead anymore...
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: How do, we go—go back?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Rankin, tell me...
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: How do, we live?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Vetochki.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: No. The way you, call me. You're treating me, like a child. I-I'm not.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: You're not, talking back.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: You just proved what I had only guessed.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: What?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: All I've done up to this point was to force my ideas on you. You didn't understand my plan, you didn't understand the meaning behind my actions.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: You trusted me, not your own choices. What will you do when I'm not around to lead you?
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I appreciate your trust, but this isn't right.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: I'm going to tell you what's happening, and then you can make your choice.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: Rankin once again scans each face before him, making sure that he has everyone's undivided attention.
Our ultimate goal is to reach the Kazimierzian border, and find the village of the Infected. In the process, though, this journey must become a reason for Ursus to recognize us.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: But it will put us in grave danger, because it challenges the very foundation of the Empire.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: People before us have tried and failed, leaving behind nothing but a symbol and a name, on this snowy, swampy tundra.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: What if I told you that following me might mean following in their footsteps? It's not something I would recommend for anyone who wants to live, anyone with a brain.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: But I never even gave Hawley a choice. That's why he's dead.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: He showed me that I must return the choice to you.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: The crowd does not answer, but there is some faint commotion.
Slowly, the crowd begins to split. Some stand next to Rankin or behind him, others in front of him.
When do we leave, Rankin?
attributes.name: Calm Miner

attributes.content: Once everyone has made their choice.
attributes.name: Rankin

attributes.content: More and more people move towards Rankin, their footsteps like the delayed ringing of the bell, announcing the end of a shift.
One, two, three... one miscount may mean one more soul lost to the mines.
Who doesn't, want to live?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Why do we, have to choose?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Vetochki can feel them growing more and more distant.
She subconsciously takes a step forward, trying to hold on to something, but someone grabs her and stops her in her tracks.
—Vetochki.
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: S-Serafina?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Y-You're going too? Where were you? I-I don't know, how to...
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Th-They're all leaving! Leaving us to, go die!
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: It's alright, it's alright. I was just bandaging up Nikto, he cut himself on some broken mirror shards—
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: It's not important. I heard the last part of your exchange. You want to live. There's nothing wrong with that. You don't have to make yourself walk a path that you don't agree with, and I'm not going with them.
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: Y-You won't? You're not, leaving?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Yes, me and my team will continue to assist the wounded. Nikto is not leaving either, is he?
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: ...Nikto?
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: The split crowd continues to move. Raidian could not see Nikto, so she begins scanning for a specific electrical signal with her Arts. It does not take her long.
He's in the rear. He won't leave—
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: I'm sorry, Gospozha Serafina.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Nikto—
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: I'm sorry, Vetochki.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: I can't go back. You know that I came from Grypherburg. The mines were never my home.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: Besides, it's not a sure thing that we'll die. Maybe things won't get so bad with me keeping an eye on the man.
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: We must make opportunity with our own hands, after all, right?
attributes.name: Nikto

attributes.content: No. No, no, no!
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Why... why?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Why must, it be, like this?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Why must we... always...
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: ...part?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Go home, Vetochki. Don't catch a cold.
attributes.name: Gentle Voice

attributes.content: Go to bed, and mom and dad will be back when you wake up again.
attributes.name: Gentle Voice

attributes.content: Be a good girl who doesn't cry or complain, make your babushka happy... and you'll find a piece of chocolate on your pillow when you wake up!
attributes.name: Gentle Voice

attributes.content: ...Strike...
attributes.name: Indistinct Voice

attributes.content: Rights... no... response...
attributes.name: Indistinct Voice

attributes.content: ...Execution...
attributes.name: Indistinct Voice

attributes.content: 'Mama.'
attributes.name: Strange Voice

attributes.content: 'Papa.'
attributes.name: Strange Voice

attributes.content: 'Ditya.'
attributes.name: Strange Voice

attributes.content: ...'Babushka?'
attributes.name: Strange Voice

attributes.content: (Hums)
attributes.name: 'Babushka'

attributes.content: One word! Just one word!
attributes.name: Anxious Voice

attributes.content: One word, and my soul will be healed!
attributes.name: Anxious Voice

attributes.content: (Hums)
attributes.name: 'Babushka'

attributes.content: —I beg forgiveness... for the sin...
attributes.name: Anxious Voice

attributes.content: Of killing your child...
attributes.name: Anxious Voice

attributes.content: 'I shall...'
attributes.name: 'Babushka'

attributes.text: 'I shall meditate and share in your pain.'
'For you, for him. May we all repent our sins from the bottom of our hearts.'
'Thy blessings walk, on earth as it is in heaven.'

attributes.content: ...Babushka?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: (Hums)
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: ...Babushka...
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: (Hums)
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: Babushka... is someone, outside?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: No one stops, no one waits.
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: They're... gone.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: They cry, kiss your hand, say they didn't, want to be apart... but they're gone.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Everyone has their own path.
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: Must this be? But, why...
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: You know the reason, suffering soul.
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: Rankin... never taught me... how to, read that watch.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Second hand, clock...? I can hear, the bell.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: As always.
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: Babushka... why is your hand so cold?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: The fire is hot.
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: I'm cold... and it hurts...
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: But it's not... because of, fire.
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: I don't understand...
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: It's alright. Most people live their entire lives in obliviousness.
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: Until... they die?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Yes, until they die.
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: (Hums)
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: Babushka... what are you, singing about?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Love.
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: But I... hurt?
attributes.name: Vetochki

attributes.content: Because you love.
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: Love makes people hurt, makes them persevere, makes them docile.
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: Love makes people strong, makes them wealthy, makes them noble.
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: You love, and therefore you hurt. I love you, and therefore I share in your pain.
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: Vetochki says no more. She closes her eyes and curls up tightly, but no longer dares touch the hand that caresses her cheek as usual.
She had heard these words before, back when she did not need to find support for them amongst broken pieces. Now, though, she is no longer sure.
How can one be more wealthy and noble than kings and nobles if one lives in obliviousness?
How can one be stronger than the stick of authority if one is powerless?
The campfire is very near. It should have brought warmth, but Vetochki feels cold in her limbs, her innards, every part of her body—
Passing through the cold, withered hand.
'I shall meditate and share in your pain.'
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: 'May we all...'
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: '...repent our sins from the bottom of our hearts.'
attributes.name: Nadia

attributes.content: The voice is simply a repetition, an echo.
The echo points towards... doom.
